transparent}#wm-ipp{z-index:2147483647}#wm-ipp,#wm-ipp *{font-family:Lucida Grande,Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;font-size:12px;line-height:1.2;letter-spacing:0;width:auto;height:auto;max-width:none;max-height:none;min-width:0!important;min-height:0;outline:none;float:none;text-align:left;border:none;color:#000;text-indent:0;position:initial;background:none}#wm-ipp div,#wm-ipp canvas{display:block}#wm-ipp div,#wm-ipp tr,#wm-ipp td,#wm-ipp a,#wm-ipp form{padding:0;margin:0;border:none;border-radius:0;background-color:transparent;background-image:none;height:auto}#wm-ipp table{border:none;border-collapse:collapse;margin:0;padding:0;width:auto;font-size:inherit}#wm-ipp form input{padding:1px!important;height:auto;display:inline;margin:0;color:#000;background:none #fff;border:1px solid #666}#wm-ipp form input[type=submit]{padding:0 8px!important;margin:1px 0 1px 5px!important;width:auto!important;border:1px solid #000!important;background:#fff!important;color:#000!important}#wm-ipp form input[type=submit]:hover{background:#eee!important;cursor:pointer!important}#wm-ipp form input[type=submit]:active{transform:translateY(1px)}#wm-ipp a{display:inline}#wm-ipp a:hover{text-decoration:underline}#wm-ipp a.wm-btn:hover{text-decoration:none;color:#ff0!important}#wm-ipp a.wm-btn:hover span{color:#ff0!important}#wm-ipp #wm-ipp-inside{margin:0 6px;border:5px solid #000;border-top:none;background-color:rgba(255,255,255,0.9);-moz-box-shadow:1px 1px 4px #333;-webkit-box-shadow:1px 1px 4px #333;box-shadow:1px 1px 4px #333;border-radius:0 0 8px 8px}#wm-ipp #wm-logo{padding:0 10px;vertical-align:middle;flex:0 0 100px}#wm-ipp .c{padding-left:4px}#wm-ipp .c .u{margin-top:4px!important}#wm-ipp .n{padding:0 0 0 5px!important;vertical-align:bottom}#wm-ipp .n a{text-decoration:none;color:#33f;font-weight:bold}#wm-ipp .n .b{padding:0 6px 0 0!important;text-align:right!important;overflow:visible;white-space:nowrap;color:#99a;vertical-align:middle}#wm-ipp .n .y .b{padding:0 6px 2px 0!important}#wm-ipp .n .c{background:#000;color:#ff0;font-weight:bold;padding:0!important;text-align:center}#wm-ipp .n .d span.ta{display:inline-block;width:0;height:0;border-color:transparent #99a;border-style:solid}#wm-ipp .n .d .b span.ta{border-width:8px 14px 8px 0}#wm-ipp .n .d .f span.ta{border-width:8px 0 8px 14px}#wm-ipp .n .d a span.ta{border-color:transparent #33f}#wm-ipp .n .d a span.ta:hover{border-color:transparent #77f}#wm-ipp .n td.f{padding:0 0 0 6px!important;text-align:left!important;overflow:visible;white-space:nowrap;color:#99a;vertical-align:middle}#wm-ipp .n tr.m td{text-transform:uppercase;white-space:nowrap}#wm-ipp .c .s{vertical-align:bottom}#wm-ipp #wm-nav-captures{white-space:nowrap}#wm-ipp .c .s a.t{color:#33f;font-weight:bold;line-height:1.8}#wm-ipp .c .s div.r{color:#666;font-size:9px;white-space:nowrap}#wm-ipp .c .k{padding-bottom:1px}#wm-ipp .c .s{padding:0 5px 2px 0!important}#wm-ipp td#displayMonthEl{padding:2px 0!important}#wm-ipp td#displayYearEl{padding:0 0 2px 0!important}div#wm-ipp-sparkline{white-space:nowrap;background-color:#fff;cursor:pointer;line-height:0.9}#wm-sparkline-canvas{position:relative;z-index:9012;max-width:none}#wm-ipp-sparkline .r{margin-left:4px}#wm-ipp .r a{color:#33f;border:none;position:relative;background-color:transparent;background-repeat:no-repeat!important;background-position:100% #wm-expand{right:1px;bottom:-1px;color:#ffffff;background-color:#666!important;padding:0 5px 0 3px!important;border-radius:3px 3px 0 0!important}#wm-ipp #wm-expand span{color:#ffffff}#wm-ipp #wm-expand #wm-expand-icon{display:inline-block;transition:transform 0.5s;transform-origin:50% 45%}#wm-ipp #wmtb{text-align:right}#wm-ipp #wmtb #wmtbURL{width:calc(100% - 45px)}#wm-ipp ntent:"♫"}.iconochive-No_Flag:before{content:"⚐"}.iconochive-Flag:before{content:"⚑"}.iconochive-Done:before{content:"✓"}.iconochive-Check:before{content:"✔"}.iconochive-Refresh:before{content:"⟳"}.iconochive-Headphones:before{content:"🎧"}.iconochive-Chart:before{content:"📈"}.iconochive-Bookmark:before{content:"📑"}.iconochive-Documents:before{content:"📚"}.iconochive-Newspaper:before{content:"📰"}.iconochive-Podcast:before{content:"📶"}.iconochive-Radio:before{content:"📻"}.iconochive-Cassette:before{content:"📼"}.iconochive-Shuffle:before{content:"🔀"}.iconochive-Loop:before{content:"🔁"}.iconochive-Low_Audio:before{content:"🔉"}.iconochive-First:before{content:"🎖"}.iconochive-Invisible:before{content:"🕶"}.iconochive-Computer:before{content:"🖳"}</style>

7.2. Review Mode — Felix Manual 1.7.1.1 documentation

a{text-decoration:underline}.clearer{clear:both}.left{float:left}.right{float:right}h1,h2,h3{font-family:"Georgia","Times New Roman",serif;font-weight:normal;color:#3465a4;margin-bottom:.8em}h1{color:#204a87}h2{padding-bottom:.5em;border-bottom:1px solid #3465a4}a.headerlink{visibility:hidden;color:#dddddd;padding-left:.3em}h1:hover>a.headerlink,h2:hover>a.headerlink,h3:hover>a.headerlink,h4:hover>a.headerlink,h5:hover>a.headerlink,h6:hover>a.headerlink,dt:hover>a.headerlink{visibility:visible}img{border:0}div.admonition{font-size:0.9em;margin:1em .headertitle{font-family:"Georgia","Times New Roman",serif;font-weight:normal;font-size:180%;letter-spacing:.08em;margin-bottom:.8em}div.header .headertitle a{color:white}div.header div.rel{margin-top:1em}div.header div.rel a{color:#fcaf3e;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase}div.content-wrapper{background-color:white;padding-top:20px;padding-bottom:20px}div.document{width:50em;float:left}div.body{padding-right:2em;text-align:justify}div.document h1{line-height:120%}div.document .section{margin-top:1.7em}div.document .section:first-child{margin-top:0px}div.document h2{margin-top:.7em}div.document p{margin-bottom:.5em}div.document .docutils.literal{background-color:#eeeeec;padding:1px}div.sidebar{width:20em;float:right;font-size:.9em}div.sidebar a,div.header a{text-decoration:none}div.sidebar a:hover,div.header a:hover{text-decoration:underline}div.sidebar h3{color:#2e3436;text-transform:uppercase;font-size:130%;letter-spacing:.1em}div.sidebar ul{list-style-type:none}div.sidebar li.toctree-l1 a{display:block;padding:1px;border:1px solid #dddddd;background-color:#eeeeec;margin-bottom:.4em;padding-left:3px;color:#2e3436}div.sidebar li.toctree-l2 a{background-color:transparent;border:none;margin-left:1em;border-bottom:1px solid #dddddd}div.sidebar li.toctree-l2:last-child a{border-bottom:none}div.sidebar li.toctree-l1.current a{border-right:5px solid #fcaf3e}div.sidebar li.toctree-l1.current li.toctree-l2 a{border-right:none}div.sidebar input[type="text"]{width:170px}div.sidebar input[type="submit"]{width:30px}div.footer-wrapper{background:url(data:,)top left repeat-x;border-top:4px solid #babdb6;padding-top:10px;padding-bottom:10px;min-height:80px}div.footer,div.footer a{color:#888a85}div.footer .right{text-align:right}div.footer .left{text-transform:uppercase}.figure{margin-top:1em;margin-bottom:1em}.caption{margin-top:.5em;margin-left:1em;font-style:italic}div.viewcode-block:target{margin:-1px -3px;padding:0 3px;background-color:#f4debf;border-top:1px solid #ac9;border-bottom:1px solid #ac9}</style>

<div class="header">

<div class="headertitle">

[Felix Manual 1.7.1.1 documentation](https://web.archive.org/web/20181018011016/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/index.html)

</div>

<div class="rel">

<a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181018011016/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/word/trans-mode.html" accesskey="P" title="7.1. Translation Mode">previous</a> \| <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181018011016/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/word/preferences.html" accesskey="N" title="7.3. Setting Preferences">next</a> \| <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181018011016/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/genindex.html" accesskey="I" title="General Index">index</a>

<div class="content-wrapper">

<div class="content">

<div class="document">

<div class="documentwrapper">

<div class="bodywrapper">

<div class="body">

<div id="review-mode" class="section">

<span id="word-review-mode"></span><span id="index-0"></span>

# 7.2. Review Mode<a href="#review-mode" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

Use Review Mode to review your translation using Felix. Review Mode essentially functions like Translation Mode (see <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181018011016/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/word/trans-mode.html#word-trans-mode" class="reference internal"><em>Translation Mode</em></a>), except that instead of looking up source text to translate, you are looking up translations for review.

To enter review mode, click the half-green, half-white toolbar button on the Felix toolbar.

![Switch to review mode toolbar button](data:image/png;base64,)

In Review mode, the colors of the toolbar buttons are reversed.

![Review mode toolbar](data:image/png;base64,)

<div class="admonition note">

Note

In Word 2007 and later, the menu and toolbar appear in the Add-Ins tab. In Word 2003, an asterisk in square brackets (\[\*\]) appears to the right of the menu item. This helps you tell that you are in Review Mode.

</div>

Each of the Review Mode functions is described below.

<div id="look-up-a-translation" class="section">

<span id="word-review-lookup"></span>

## 7.2.1. Look up a Translation<a href="#look-up-a-translation" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

To have the suite automatically select the next translation to look up, place the cursor in front of the sentence, and select *Felix ‣ Find Next Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`→`</span>). The Felix interface will skip any whitespace before the next sentence, and select all the text up to the next tab, line break, or end-of-sentence marker (e.g. period). The Felix interface will then send the sentence to Felix for lookup.

Most text formatting information (e.g. bold, italic, font, text color) is preserved in the query. Some formatting information, however, such as highlight color and centered text, is not preserved.

To select the sentence to look up yourself, select a segment of text, then select *Felix ‣ Find Current Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`L`</span>).

If you would like to extend the lookup segment beyond the current selection, then with the current lookup scope selected, select *Felix ‣ Extend Lookup Sentence* (or press <span class="pre">`Ctrl`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`→`</span>).

Note that unlike Translation Mode, the Query is the translation segment, not the source-language segment. Additionally, the match score is for its similarity with the Trans field of the memory entry, rather than the Source field. See below for a screen shot.

</div>

<div id="navigate-through-the-matches" class="section">

<span id="word-review-nav"></span>

## 7.2.2. Navigate through the Matches<a href="#navigate-through-the-matches" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

Sometimes when you look up a translation in your memory, Felix will have more than one suggestion. The number of suggestions, and the number of the current suggestion, will be shown at the bottom right of the suggestion:

<div class="figure">

![Fuzzy match view in Felix](data:image/png;base64,)

Fuzzy match view in Felix

</div>

In this example, the first of two suggestions (1/2) is being shown.

To view the next suggestion, click on Next in the Memory window, or from Word, select *Felix ‣ Next Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`N`</span>).

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

The score for the next translation (in this example 68%) is shown next to the Next link.

</div>

To view the previous suggestion, click on Prev in the Memory window, or from Word, select *Felix ‣ Previous Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`P`</span>).

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

The score for the previous translation (in this example 68%) is shown next to the Prev link.

</div>

Matches will cycle: If you press Next while on the last suggestion, the first suggestion will be shown; likewise, pressing Prev while on the first suggestion will show the last suggestion.

</div>

<div id="correct-a-translation" class="section">

<span id="word-review-correct"></span>

## 7.2.3. Correct a Translation<a href="#correct-a-translation" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

While a translation query is shown in the Memory window (see 5.3.2 Look up a Translation, above), place the cursor at the end of your translation, and select *Felix ‣ Correct Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`↑`</span>). The segment from the start of the original sentence to the current cursor position is registered as the translation.

To manually select the segment to register as the translation, select a segment of text, then select *Felix ‣ Correct Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`↑`</span>). The current selection is registered as the translation.

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

Select *Felix ‣ Correct And Next* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`S`</span>) to register the current translation, then automatically select and look up the next sentence. This is equivalent to pressing <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`↑`</span>, then <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`→`</span>.

</div>

</div>

<div id="add-a-glossary-entry" class="section">

<span id="word-review-add-gloss"></span>

## 7.2.4. Add a Glossary Entry<a href="#add-a-glossary-entry" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

While you are reviewing, you may want to add terms to your glossary. You can do this easily from inside Word. Simply select the translation of your glossary term, and from the Felix menu, select “Add to Glossary”. Alternatively, you can press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`M,`</span>` `<span class="pre">`G`</span>.

The Add to Glossary dialog box appears. Type in a source for the translation, and click Add. The entry is added to the Felix glossary.

You can find more detailed instructions, including screen shots, here.

</div>

<div id="delete-a-translation" class="section">

<span id="word-review-delete"></span>

## 7.2.5. Delete a Translation<a href="#delete-a-translation" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

While a suggestion is being shown in the Memory window (see 5.3.2 Look up a Translation, above), select *Felix ‣ Delete Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`D`</span>), or in the Felix window, press the Delete link.

A message box will appear, asking if you really want to delete this translation. To delete the translation, click Yes. The currently displayed entry is deleted. If you change your mind, click No . The entry will not be deleted.

</div>

<div id="restore-from-memory" class="section">

<span id="word-review-restore"></span>

## 7.2.6. Restore from Memory<a href="#restore-from-memory" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

After you have edited a translation, you may wish to restore the translation stored in the memory, or you may wish to replace the current translation with another from memory.

While a suggestion is being shown in the Memory window (see 5.3.2 Look up a Translation, above), select *Felix ‣ Restore Translation* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`↓`</span>). The current selection is replaced by the suggestion.

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

Select *Felix ‣ Restore And Lookup Next Trans* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`G`</span>) to restore the current translation, and automatically select and look up the next translation. This is equivalent to pressing <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`↓`</span>, <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`↑`</span>, then <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`→`</span>.

</div>

</div>

<div id="get-a-glossary-entry" class="section">

<span id="word-review-gloss"></span>

## 7.2.7. Get a Glossary Entry<a href="#get-a-glossary-entry" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

Every time you look up a translation in the memory, the source field of the current match is also searched for matches in all open glossaries. Any matches are displayed in the corresponding Glossary window. This helps you ensure that you are using the technical terms contained in your glossaries, especially if you have mandatory terminology guidelines from your client.

<div class="figure">

![Glossary Search Results View](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Glossary Search Results View

</div>

As you are typing, you can retrieve any matches shown in the Main Glossary window via hot keys. Select *Felix ‣ Get Glossary Entry ‣ Entry {N}* (or press <span class="pre">`ALT`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`{N}`</span>), where {N} is a number between 0 and 9, to retrieve that glossary entry. If the number of the entry you wish to retrieve is higher than 9, then press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`M,`</span>` `<span class="pre">`H`</span> (Note that this will not function if the IME is turned on). An input box appears; enter the entry number and press Return to retrieve the corresponding entry.

</div>

<div id="search-for-concordance" class="section">

<span id="word-review-concordance"></span>

## 7.2.8. Search for Concordance<a href="#search-for-concordance" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

Oftentimes as you review your translation, you will wonder how you have used a particular term in previous translations. This is what the Concordance feature is for. Select the term you wish to investigate, then select *Felix ‣ Find Concordance* (or press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`M,`</span>` `<span class="pre">`C`</span>). Any entries in the memory containing that term in the Trans field appear in the Memory window: Concordance Results View

</div>

<div id="save-the-memory" class="section">

<span id="word-review-save"></span>

## 7.2.9. Save the Memory<a href="#save-the-memory" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

A prudent backup strategy will save you future headaches in any computing situation, and Felix is no exception. In order to facilitate saving the memory and glossaries, you can perform this from Word via a keyboard shortcut: Just press <span class="pre">`Alt`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`M,`</span>` `<span class="pre">`S`</span> to save your current memory and glossaries (Note that this will not function if the IME is turned on). You can also do this from the menu, by selecting *Felix ‣ Save Memory*.

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

Enter search terms or a module, class or function name.
